case_title: MK ENTERPRISES INC. VS. JAMES EASON

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MK ENTERPRISES, INC'S PETITION (VERIFIED) FOR AN ORDER VACATING ARBITRATION AWARD. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PETITIONER'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT ACT IN EXCESS OF HER POWERS. MOREOVER, THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT IMPROPERLY EXCLUDE EVIDENCE. RESPONDENT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE REQUEST WAS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER. RESPONDENT MUST FILE A SEPARATE MOTION FOR FEES AND COSTS AFTER THE COURT HEARS AND GRANTS ITS PETITION TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR RESPONDENT JAMES EASON'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS HEARING SET FOR JAN-18-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT JAMES EASON'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED AS TO COSTS OF $450 AND DENIED AS TO ATTORNEY'S FEES. AS THE PREVAILING ON THE VALIDITY OF THE ARBITRATION AWARD, RESPONDENT IS ENTITLED TO HIS COSTS FOR HIS FIRST APPEARANCE FEE AND THE STIPULATION (BUT NOT THIS MOTION. ATTORNEY'S FEES ARE ONLY PERMITTED AS COSTS WHEN AUTHORIZED BY CONTRACT OR STATUTE. THE PARTIES' ARBITRATION AGREEMENT DOES NOT PROVIDE THAT A COURT CAN MAKE AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND RESPONDENT DOES NOT CITE TO ANY STATUTE SO PROVIDING.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

calendar.matters:
Respondent James Eason'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jan-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KENDRICK D. NGUYEN OF MK ENTERPRISES, INC, TEL. NO. 931-9292 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. MARK J. BLUER OF BLUER & BLUER, LLP, TEL. NO. 989-1281 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Respondent James Eason'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs is Granted as to costs of $450 and denied as to attorney's fees. As the prevailing on the validity of the arbitration award, respondent is entitled to his costs for his first appearance fee and the stipulation (but not this motion. Attorney's fees are only permitted as costs when authorized by contract or statute. The parties' arbitration agreement does not provide that a court can make an award of attorney's fees and respondent does not cite to any statute so providing.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jan-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court vacated its tentative ruling, and RESPONDENT JAMES EASON's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs is continued to 1/18/12 per e-mail sent to the court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Vacating Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER MK ENTERPRISES, INC.'S PETITION (VERIFIED) FOR AN ORDER VACATING ARBITRATION AWARD.

APPEARANCES: Jody Weiner, Esq. of Jody Weiner & Associates 921-2042 for petitioner & moving party; Mark J. Bluer, Esq. of Bluer & Bluer, LLP 989-1281 for respondent & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - PETITIONER'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT ACT IN EXCESS OF HER POWERS. MOREOVER, THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT IMPROPERLY EXCLUDE EVIDENCE. RESPONDENT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE REQUEST WAS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER. RESPONDENT MUST FILE A SEPARATE MOTION FOR FEES AND COSTS AFTER THE COURT HEARS AND GRANTS ITS PETITION TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
